examples: The proportions of flavin, non-heme iron and labile sulphide in the eluate was found to be 1: (8~10) :10.
FMN (flavin mononucleotide) A derivative of riboflavin that is a coenzyme in electron-transfer reactions.
Dihydrolipoamide dehydrogenase (DLDH), a flavin-dependent oxidoreductase, is an ubiquitous enzyme which presents in variety of organisms and plays an important role in energy metabolism.
Collectors can view a video contribution by Bill Viola, minimalist installations by Dan Flavin, Donald Judd and Sol Lewitt.
Flavin dabbled in Abstract Expressionism himself before turning to Minimalism and, ultimately, his signature fluorescent motif in the 1960s.
